series.title: Journey to the West
series.description: Follow the Monkey King, Tang Sanzang, Zhu Bajie and Sha Wujing on their legendary journey. Each part continues the story.

parts.title: Part 1
parts.transcript:
(00:11) 西遊記 一：石猴出世。
(00:16) 很久很久以前，有一座山。
(00:28) 山頂上有塊石頭。
(00:30) 天地日月每天都對這塊石頭施展著法術。
(00:39) 有一天，這塊石頭裂開了。
(00:44) 一隻猴子躺在石頭裡。
(00:50) 猴子站起身來，舒展著身體。
(00:58) 突然，猴子的雙眼射出了強光。
(01:01) 不一會，這道光就照到了天庭的靈霄寶殿。
(01:09) 天庭的最高統治者玉帝坐起身來，他扭頭看著站在自己身邊的一個天將。
(01:15) 「去看看到底哪裡來的光。」玉帝說。
(01:23) 「遵命，陛下。」天將回答。
(01:26) 天將快速走出了靈霄寶殿。
(01:32) 他一路衝出了南天門，向下界看去。
(01:38) 結果看到了那隻猴子。
(01:41) 那道光不見了，但這位天將的眼睛有法力。
(01:44) 他可以看到之前發生的一切。
(01:53) 「有意思。」天將說。
(01:56) 「怎麼了？」天將回到天宮時，玉帝問道。
(02:01) 「那光是從哪裡來的？」
(02:03) 「是一隻猴子弄出來的。」天將回答。
(02:09) 「猴子？」玉帝問。
(02:12) 「是的，」天將回答道：「他在人間的花果山上。」
(02:17) 「那山上有一塊石頭，那猴子就是從石頭裡蹦出來的。」
(02:24) 「那道光就是從猴子的眼睛裡發出來的。」
(02:32) 玉帝想了一會，然後聳聳肩。
(02:35) 「那我倒覺得，」他說。
(02:38) 「天地合之時，總有奇妙的事情發生。」
(02:48) 日子一天天過去，這隻猴子學會了走、跑和爬。
(02:56) 他喝的是小溪水，吃的是草和樹葉。
(03:08) 他採果子，在山洞和山頂探險。
(03:22) 他成了其他所有猴子的朋友。
(03:30) 到了晚上，他就在一個懸崖下睡覺。
(03:34) 有一天早上，天氣非常熱，所有的猴子都在樹陰裡玩耍。
(03:37) 「今天太熱了！」有隻猴子叫道。
(03:44) 「我們去小溪裡玩吧！」
(03:47) 「好主意！」另一隻猴子回答道。
(03:51) 一群猴子跑到小溪邊。
(03:56) 水流衝擊著石頭，激起了朵朵水花。
(03:59) 猴子們跳進了清涼的溪水裡。
(04:06) 所有猴子都在水裡玩耍。
(04:08) 有隻猴子撓了撓腦袋。
(04:11) 「我們都喜歡這條小溪，」他說。
(04:16) 「我們喝小溪的水，我們在溪裡游泳，我們知道它一路流到海裡去。」
(04:22) 「但有誰知道這條小溪的源頭在哪裡嗎？」
(04:28) 猴子們你看我、我看你，都搖頭。
(04:32) 「我們今天沒什麼事情做，」
(04:37) 那隻猴子繼續說道：「不如我們沿著小溪走，去找找它的源頭。」
(04:43) 「聽上去挺有趣的，」另一隻猴子說。
(04:50) 「那我們走吧！」第三隻猴子說。
(04:57) 猴子們都從水裡跳了出來。
(05:00) 他們沿著小溪一路走，從這棵樹蕩到那棵樹上。
(05:03) 他們跳過一塊塊岩石，向山上爬去。
(05:07) 他們越爬越高，最後在一處巨大的瀑布前停住了。
(05:15) 空氣裡有很多水霧，水流下來發出巨大的聲音。
(05:24) 「我想知道那瀑布後面是什麼。」有隻猴子說。
(05:31) 「我也想知道，」另一隻猴子說：「但沒人能穿過瀑布啊。」
(05:38) 「只要誰可以穿過瀑布再活著回來，我們就封他為王！」一隻長臂猿說。
(05:47) 「有人敢去試試嗎？」
(05:53) 猴子們你看我、我看你，都搖著頭。
(05:59) 「我不敢試。」一隻高高的猴子說。
(06:02) 「我也不敢。」一隻矮猴子說。
(06:07) 有人喊道：「我來！」
(06:15) 猴子們紛紛給那個說話的猴子讓出一條道來。
(06:18) 是那隻石頭裡蹦出來的猴子。
(06:22) 「你確定要試試嗎？」長臂猿問：「這看上去挺危險的。」
(06:30) 石猴笑了：「我什麼都不怕！我回來的時候，就是你們的大王了！」
(06:36) 做了一個深呼吸，
(06:39) 跳進了瀑布裡。
parts.vocabs:
Key Characters and Titles
西遊記 (Xīyóujì): Journey to the West
石猴 (Shíhóu): Stone Monkey
玉帝 (Yùdì): Jade Emperor
天將 (Tiānjiàng): Heavenly General
陛下 (Bìxià): Your Majesty
王 (Wáng) / 大王 (Dàwáng): King / Great King
長臂猿 (Chángbìyuán): Gibbon (long-armed ape)

Locations and Nature
山頂 (Shāndǐng): Mountain top
天地 (Tiāndì): Heaven and Earth
日月 (Rìyuè): Sun and Moon
天庭 (Tiāntíng) / 天宮 (Tiāngōng): Heavenly Court / Heavenly Palace
靈霄寶殿 (Língxiāo Bǎodiàn): Hall of Divine Mists
南天門 (Nántiānmén): Southern Heavenly Gate
下界 (Xiàjiè): The mortal world
花果山 (Huāguǒ Shān): Mountain of Flowers and Fruits
小溪 (Xiǎoxī): Small stream
山洞 (Shāndòng): Cave
懸崖 (Xuányá): Cliff
樹蔭 (Shùyīn): Shade of a tree
瀑布 (Pùbù): Waterfall
源頭 (Yuántóu): Source (of a stream)

Objects and Concepts
石頭 (Shítóu): Stone
法術 (Fǎshù): Magic / Spells
強光 (Qiángguāng): Bright light
法力 (Fǎlì): Magical power
草 (Cǎo): Grass
樹葉 (Shùyè): Tree leaves
果子 (Guǒzi): Fruit
水花 (Shuǐhuā): Water spray / splashes
岩石 (Yánshí): Rocks
水霧 (Shuǐwù): Water mist

Actions and Verbs
出世 (Chūshì): To be born / To come into the world
施展 (Shīzhǎn): To exercise / To display (magic)
裂開 (Lièkāi): To split open
躺 (Tǎng): To lie down
站 (Zhàn): To stand up
舒展 (Shūzhǎn): To stretch
射出 (Shèchū): To shoot out / To emit
蹦 (Bèng): To jump out / To burst forth
發生 (Fāshēng): To happen
走 (Zǒu) / 跑 (Pǎo) / 爬 (Pá): To walk, run and climb
喝 (Hē) / 吃 (Chī): To drink and eat
摘 (Zhāi): To pick (fruit)
探險 (Tànxiǎn): To explore / To adventure
睡覺 (Shuìjiào): To sleep
玩耍 (Wánshuǎ): To play
撓 (Náo): To scratch
游泳 (Yóuyǒng): To swim
穿過 (Chuānguò): To pass through
試 (Shì): To try
呼吸 (Hūxī): To breathe
跳 (Tiào): To jump

Descriptive Terms
很久很久以前 (Hěnjiǔ hěnjiǔ yǐqián): Long, long ago
突然 (Tūrán): Suddenly
奇妙 (Qímiào): Wonderful / Marvelous
清涼 (Qīngliáng): Cool and refreshing
巨大 (Jùdà): Huge
有趣 (Yǒuqù): Interesting / Fun
危險 (Wēixiǎn): Dangerous
parts.fullEnglish:
Part 1: The Birth of the Stone Monkey

Long, long ago, there was a mountain with a stone on its peak. Every day, the heaven, earth, sun, and moon cast magic upon this stone. One day, the stone split open, and a monkey lying inside stood up and stretched. Suddenly, the monkey's eyes emitted a strong light that reached the Hall of Divine Mists in the Heavenly Court.

The Jade Emperor, the supreme ruler of Heaven, noticed the light and ordered a Heavenly General to investigate. The General rushed out of the Southern Heavenly Gate and looked down at the mortal world. Although the light had vanished, the General's magical eyes allowed him to see what had happened. He returned to the Heavenly Palace and reported to the Jade Emperor that the light came from a monkey.

Part 2: Life on Flower Fruit Mountain

The General explained that the monkey had burst out of a stone on Flower Fruit Mountain. The Jade Emperor shrugged, noting that wonderful things happen when Heaven and Earth combine. As time passed, the monkey learned to walk, run, and climb. He drank from streams, ate grass and leaves, picked fruit, and explored caves and mountain tops. He became friends with all the other monkeys and slept under a cliff at night.

One very hot morning, the monkeys were playing in the shade. One monkey suggested going to the stream to play, and the whole group ran to the water where they jumped into the cool spray.

Part 3: The Search for the Source

While playing, one monkey wondered where the stream originated. Since they had nothing else to do, they decided to follow the stream to find its source. They swung from trees and jumped over rocks as they climbed higher up the mountain. Eventually, they reached a huge waterfall that created a thick mist and a deafening roar. The monkeys wondered what was behind the waterfall, but no one dared to try and pass through it.

Part 4: The Challenge for the Throne

A gibbon announced a challenge: whoever could pass through the waterfall and return alive would be crowned their King. The monkeys looked at each other, but most were too afraid to try.

Suddenly, the monkey born from the stone shouted, "I'll do it!" Despite being warned of the danger, the Stone Monkey laughed and said he feared nothing, declaring he would be their Great King when he returned. He took a deep breath and jumped into the waterfall.
